jobs.title: Senior Director of Operations, Los Angeles
jobs.description:
**Summary:**


Launched in 2014, the ASPCA Los Angeles Initiative layers policy work, inter\-agency partnerships and direct service programs to drive change for animals and the community. The Los Angeles Initiative is comprised of 80 people from more than a dozen departments, including annual grants of approximately $1 million. In addition to policy work, current direct services include kitten intake diversion, foster and adoption; animal relocation; and veterinary services (spay/neuter and primary pet care).


The ASPCA’s Los Angeles work is crucially dependent on seamless collaboration and alignment both within the team and with our community partners, which include municipal shelters, non\-profits and policy makers.


The Senior Director of Shelter Operations (SD, Shelter Operations), reporting to the Senior Director of the Los Angeles Initiative, is assigned primarily to support the ASPCA’s partnership work with Los Angeles City Animal Services (LAAS) and as such will work with LAAS leadership on\-site at the shelter locations. The focus of this work is the implementation of a joint 3\-year plan for LAAS shelters, led by LAAS, and resourced and directly supported by the ASPCA and Best Friends. This work aims to achieve the following goals at LAAS shelters:


* intake community engagement, including efforts to support community cats;
* in\-shelter pathway planning to ensure animals receive timely, coordinated care and support, and that the shelters are operating within capacity for humane care; and
* positive outcomes for animals by increasing adoptions, transfers, and other lifesaving placements

**Who We Are**


The LA Initiative is seated within the Shelter Partnerships Department. The Shelter Partnership Department executes the ASPCA’s strategic approach by leveraging direct care, response work, and public influence to drive systemic changes that include improving animal welfare across our society. Our Shelter Partnership Team’s work focuses on strengthening shelter systems.


The LA Intiative Team leads high\-impact partnership efforts with Los Angeles area organizations, driving initiatives that improve shelter operations, enhance animal welfare, and support the achievement of key lifesaving goals. The team's work requires strong cross\-functional collaboration, relationship management, and a commitment to continuous improvement.


**What You’ll Do**


In collaboration with the LAAS General Manager and cross\-agency leadership, this position implements programs and processes in service of the above\-mentioned goals. This includes providing shelter operations subject matter expertise on shelter programs for community engagement intake, animal pathway planning, and maximizing appropriate animal placements (adoptions, relocations/transfers, etc).


Working with the Senior Director, Los Angeles Initiative, and the Sr. Dir. Shelter Medicine, LA, other members of the ASPCA’s Los Angeles team and external agency partners, led by LAAS leadership, the Sr. Dir. Ops. will also create and/or deliver trainings tailored to the needs identified within the LAAS shelter system.


The Sr. Dir. Ops., will provide iterative feedback on the workplan and its implementation, and routinely use data and analysis to inform next steps.


This position provides a strategic perspective, thought leadership, project planning skills and tactical guidance relevant to plan implementation.


The Senior Director, Shelter Operations for the Los Angeles Initiative reports directly to the Senior Director, Los Angeles Initiative and has no direct reports.


This position will be part of a team of two on\-site ASPCA staff members providing shelter operations and shelter medicine expertise to LAAS leadership.


**Where and When You’ll Work**


This position is primarily onsite, with approximately 90% of time spent across the six Los Angeles city shelters and about 10% remote work. Regular travel between LAAS shelters and the remote work location is required. This position is currently funded for 3 years


The standard schedule is Monday through Friday and generally aligns with shelter operational hours, though adjustments may be needed based on shelter needs. Scheduling will be coordinated between this role and the supervisor to ensure appropriate coverage, total hours, and a balanced workweek.


**What You’ll Get**


**Compensation**


Starting pay for the successful applicant will depend on a variety of factors, including but not limited to education, training, experience, location, business needs, internal equity, market demands or budgeted amount for the role. The target hiring range is for new hire offers only, and staff compensation may increase beyond the maximum hiring range based on performance over time. The maximum of the hiring range is reserved for candidates with the highest qualifications and relevant experience. The expected hiring salary range for this role is set forth below and may be modified in the future.


* The target hiring range for this role is $148,000 \- $160,000

**Benefits**


At the ASPCA, you don’t have to choose between your passion and making a living. Our comprehensive benefits package helps ensure you live a rewarding life at work and at home. Our benefits include, but are not limited to:


* **Affordable health coverage,** including medical, employer\-paid dental and optional vision coverage.
* **Flexible time off** that includes vacation time, sick and bereavement time, paid parental leave, 10 company paid holidays and paid personal time off that allows flexibility to observe the days that mean the most to you.
* **Competitive financial incentives and retirement savings,** including a 401(k) plan with generous employer contributions — we match dollar\-for\-dollar up to 4% and provide an additional 4% contribution toward your future each year.
* **Robust professional development opportunities,** including classes, on\-the\-job training, coaching and mentorship with industry\-leading

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Director of Development**

**The Citizen Service \| Metuchen, NJ**

**Salary: $85,000**

**About The Citizen Service (TCS)**

The Citizen Service is a national nonprofit organization dedicated to building a citizen movement that empowers people across varied backgrounds and circumstances with the tools, confidence, and skills to solve community problems that matter most to them. Through our No\-Blame, Pragmatic Problem\-Solving approach, we work with community colleges, high schools, and community organizations to develop citizen leaders who go beyond the ballot to shape the future of the places they call home.

Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Metuchen, New Jersey, The Citizen Service has spent nearly three decades proving that everyday people, given the right training and infrastructure, have real power to drive change. We are now at an exciting inflection point, with new executive leadership in place, a national expansion underway, and a landmark 30th anniversary on the horizon. This is a rare opportunity to join an organization at a pivotal moment and help build a movement that will inspire change!

**The Opportunity**

The Director of Development is a new role at The Citizen Service and a foundational hire for the organization's future. This is not a role for someone looking to maintain an existing development program. It is a role for someone who wants to build one.

The Director of Development will work closely with the Executive Director, the Founder and Board Chair, and organizational leadership to design and execute a comprehensive fundraising strategy that includes a major individual donor fundraising, a foundation and institutional grants program, and a long\-term development infrastructure that positions TCS for sustained financial health.

This role requires someone who is as comfortable working independently in an entrepreneurial environment as they are managing relationships with high\-net\-worth donors, foundation program officers, and organizational leadership. The right candidate brings campaign experience, development instincts, and the emotional intelligence to navigate a dynamic and hands\-on organization with confidence and grace.

No\-Blame Problem\-Solving is not just the work we do in communities; it is how we operate internally. We are looking for someone who brings that same spirit to their work, focusing on solutions rather than blame, approaching challenges with pragmatism and good faith, and treating colleagues as partners in figuring things out together.

**What You Will Do**

***Fundraising Campaign***

* Lead the day\-to\-day execution of a major fundraising campaign
* Develop and manage the campaign timeline, donor pipeline, and outreach strategy
* Coordinate campaign activity across the Executive Director, Founder, board members, and key stakeholders
* Develop campaign materials including the case for support, donor communications, and cultivation event collateral

***Foundation and Institutional Grants***

* Build and manage a foundation grants program from the ground up, including prospect research, LOI and proposal writing, reporting, and relationship cultivation
* Develop and maintain a grants calendar tracking foundation deadlines
* Identify foundations and institutional funders whose priorities align with TCS's strategic plan.

***Donor Relations and Stewardship***

* Manage and grow a portfolio of individual donors in partnership with the Executive Director and Founder
* Develop stewardship practices that build long\-term donor loyalty and recurring giving
* Support board members in their fundraising roles, including preparing talking points, briefing materials, and cultivation strategies
* Partner closely with the communications team to ensure email, social, and public\-facing content is aligned with and actively supports development priorities, campaign messaging, and donor cultivation efforts

***Development Infrastructure***

* Work closely with Operations to consolidate donor information currently spread across various records and systems into a single, organized donor database, an important early project that lays the foundation for everything else in this role
* Build the systems, processes, and tools that a mature development program requires including a donor database and reporting framework
* Track and report on fundraising progress to the Executive Director.
* Hire and eventually manage development support staff as the program grows

**What We Are Looking For**

We recognize that no single person will bring every quality listed below. We are not looking for a perfect match on paper. We are looking for someone eager to learn, willing to experiment, and excited to grow into this role and with this organization. If you see yourself in much of what follows, we encourage you to apply.

***Experience and Skills***

* Three to six years of nonprofit fundraising experience with demonstrated success in individual donor fundraising, grant writing, or campaign work
* Experience with or strong working knowledge of fundraising campaigns, including the ability to apply urgency and momentum to a fundraising effort
* Proven ability to build relationships with donors, the philanthropic community, and organizational stakeholders
* Strong writing skills including the ability to craft compelling donor communications and grant proposals
* Familiarity with donor databases and development tracking tools
* Comfortable building organized systems from scattered information, with the patience and attention to detail to consolidate donor records into a clean, centralized database

***Character and Approach***

* A self\-starter who thrives in entrepreneurial environments where systems are still being built and job descriptions are a starting point rather than a ceiling
* A natural practitioner of No\-Blame Problem\-Solving who approaches challenges with pragmatism, good faith, and a focus on solutions rather than blame
* Emotionally intelligent and politic

jobs.title: J.P. Morgan Wealth Management - Executive Director, Credit Decisioning Unit
jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  

  

  

Be at the center of a Line of Business wide Initiative to build a new Credit Decisioning Unit that helps manage risk and reward across bank and broker\-dealer credit products. You'll help define innovative decisioning frameworks and mature them into scalable, well\-governed processes.


In this individual contributor Executive Director role within JPMorgan Wealth Management, you will lead foundational build\-out initiatives for the Credit Decisioning Unit (a First Line Unit) and drive the transition into mature portfolio, analytics, product partnership, and governance routines. You will bring deep capital markets expertise, thrive in a fast\-paced environment, and influence across senior stakeholders, front line teams, Product, Independent Risk (2LOD), Technology, and support functions.


Job responsibilities

* Manage risk\-taking across lending portfolios using the Marginal Decisioning Framework (MDF)
* Lead the margin adequacy framework, coordinating market coverage and market\-driven actions with 1LOD and 2LOD partners
* Design and/or extend quantitative models, frameworks, and metrics, including governance where needed
* Maintain analytics quality to ensure credibility and robustness of decisioning frameworks
* Partner with front line teams and 2LOD to structure and support innovative and non\-standard asset lending solutions
* Support front line teams with complex queries, learning forums, and client discussions; publish market\-facing commentary and research as needed
* Produce compliant policies, model documentation, procedures, and reporting; manage governance, escalations, and presentations to relevant forums


Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills

* Undergraduate degree
* 12\+ years in an analytical, technical, trading, or research\-oriented role in capital markets
* Broad financial product knowledge
* Practical Python experience and familiarity with data analytics packages in a professional environment
* Practical experience with Tableau or other BI/data visualization tools and Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including explaining technical capital markets concepts to non\-technical audiences
* Team\-oriented mindset with a strong sense of ownership


Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills

* Academic concentration in a technical discipline
* Graduate degree and/or professional designations
* Background in (or experience with) financial mathematics, financial engineering, quantitative risk methodologies, and/or data science
* Experience in credit risk/market risk, derivatives/hedging strategies, credit portfolio management or lending businesses, and/or JPMorgan proprietary risk methodologies/models/frameworks


**ABOUT US**  

  

  

Chase is a leading financial services firm, helping nearly half of America's households and small businesses achieve their financial goals through a broad range of financial products. Our mission is to create engaged, lifelong relationships and put our customers at the heart of everything we do. We also help small businesses, nonprofits and cities grow, delivering solutions to solve all their financial needs.


We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission\-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on\-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.


We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.


Equal Opportunity Employer/Disability/Veterans

  

  

  

**ABOUT THE TEAM**  

  

  

Our Consumer \& Community Banking division serves our Chase customers through a range of financial services, including personal banking, credit cards, mortgages, auto financing, investment advice, small business loans and payment processing. We're proud to lead the U.S. in credit card sales and deposit growth and have the most\-used digital solutions – all while ranking first in customer satisfaction.  

  

Risk Management helps the firm understand, manage and anticipate risks in a constantly changing environment. The work covers areas such as evaluating country\-specific risk, understanding regulatory changes and determining credit worthiness. Risk Management provides independent oversight and maintains an effective control environment.

jobs.title: Director of Advancement Operations
jobs.description:
* Dobbs Ferry, NY, New York, United States
* Staff Full\-time
* $66,538 \- $86,520 annually
Position Overview



The Office of Advancement at Mercy University invites applications for a forward\-thinking, strategic, and experienced leader to serve as the Director of Advancement Operations. This critical position plays a pivotal role in driving the efficiency, innovation, and impact of our advancement initiatives, supporting fundraising, alumni engagement, and stewardship efforts that empower the mission of Mercy University. We recognize that a robust advancement operation is essential for fulfilling our mission and supporting our students, faculty, staff, and alumni.



The Director of Advancement Operations is responsible for providing strategic vision and day\-to\-day oversight of all advancement services, including gift processing, database management, prospect research, reporting and analytics, and operational policy development. The successful candidate will embrace data\-driven decision\-making, foster collaborative relationships, and model the highest standards of integrity and professionalism.



Key Responsibilities


* Develop and execute a comprehensive advancement operations strategy in alignment with organizational priorities and best practices
* Supervise, mentor, and inspire a high\-performing team of professionals across gift processing, data management, prospect research, reporting and analytics
* Serve as a critical collaborative partner to the Vice President of Institutional Advancement and other members of the Advancement team
* Foster a culture of continuous improvement, collaboration, and innovation within the Advancement Operations team and across the division


**Data Management \& Analytics**


* Oversee the integrity, security, and optimization of the advancement database and ensure best practices in data governance
* Lead the development and implementation of reporting and analytics tools to support fundraising, stewardship, and engagement programs
* Create standardized reports for Board of Trustees meetings and for University leadership
* Use data\-driven insights to identify trends, measure performance, and inform strategy for fundraising and engagement efforts


**Gift Processing \& Stewardship**


* Direct and ensure the accuracy, efficiency, and compliance of all gift processing, acknowledgment, and receipting functions
* Collaborate with finance and legal teams to align gift acceptance policies and procedures with organizational standards and IRS regulations
* Design and maintain systems to ensure donors receive timely and meaningful stewardship communications and recognition


**Prospect Research \& Pipeline Development**


* Oversee prospect research practices, ensuring the identification, qualification, and tracking of potential major donors and prospects
* Collaborate closely with fundraising teams to build and maintain robust pipelines for annual, major, and planned gifts


**Policy Development \& Compliance**


* Establish, document, and update policies and procedures to promote ethical, compliant, and transparent advancement operations
* Ensure the organization adheres to all relevant laws, regulations, and best practices in fundraising and data management


**Collaboration \& Communication**


* Serve as a key liaison to IT, Finance, Legal, and other departments, facilitating seamless integration of advancement operations across the institution
* Provide training and support to advancement staff on operational systems, tools, and policies
* Communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels, translating complex operational concepts into actionable recommendations

Qualifications


* Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred
* At least 7 years of progressive responsibility in advancement, nonprofit, or higher education operations, with at least 3 in a supervisory or management capacity
* Demonstrated expertise in data management, prospect research, gift processing, and reporting within advancement or fundraising environments
* Proven leadership skills, with experience building, mentoring, and motivating high\-performing teams
* Exceptional analytical, organizational, and project management abilities
* Strong understanding of fundraising best practices, donor stewardship, and compliance with relevant laws and regulations
* Excellent communication, interpersonal, and collaboration skills
* Proficiency with constituent relationship management (CRM) systems such as Raiser’s Edge, Salesforce, or similar platforms

Desired Attributes


* Visionary thinker with a strategic mindset and a passion for operational excellence
* Ability to navigate complex organizational structures and drive cross\-functional initiatives
* Flexible and adaptable, with a demonstrated ability to lead through change and ambiguity
* Unwavering commitment to confidentiality and ethical conduct
* Strong customer service orientation and a genuine interest in advancing the mission of Mercy University

jobs.title: Program Director
jobs.description:
**Job Overview**  
We are seeking a dynamic and energetic Program Director to lead and oversee the development, implementation, and management of impactful programs that serve individuals with developmental disabilities. This vital role requires a passionate leader committed to fostering positive outcomes through innovative program development, exceptional relationship management, and strategic oversight. As Program Director, you will inspire teams, collaborate with community partners, and ensure the delivery of high\-quality services that promote independence, well\-being, and community integration. Your leadership will be instrumental in shaping a supportive environment where both clients and staff thrive.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead the planning, development, and execution of comprehensive programs tailored to meet the needs of individuals with developmental disabilities.
* Oversee daily program operations, ensuring adherence to organizational policies, regulatory standards, and best practices in social work and behavioral health.
* Manage program budgets effectively, allocating resources efficiently while maintaining fiscal responsibility.
* Supervise program staff and direct service providers, fostering a positive team environment through mentorship, performance evaluations, and ongoing training.
* Cultivate strong relationships with clients, families, community agencies, healthcare providers, and funding sources to enhance service delivery and expand program reach.
* Develop and implement strategies for continuous quality improvement based on client feedback and outcome data.
* Ensure compliance with all licensing requirements, documentation standards, and safety protocols.
* Coordinate with external stakeholders to facilitate community engagement initiatives and advocacy efforts.
* Maintain detailed administrative records including client files, incident reports, progress notes, and program metrics.
* Lead efforts in behavioral health interventions by integrating evidence\-based practices into program offerings.

**Experience**

* Proven management experience in a social services or behavioral health setting, preferably working with individuals with developmental disabilities.
* Demonstrated success in program development from inception to execution with measurable outcomes.
* Supervisory experience leading diverse teams of professionals in a fast\-paced environment.
* Strong relationship management skills to build partnerships within the community and foster collaborative initiatives.
* Extensive administrative experience including budgeting, documentation compliance, and reporting requirements.
* Knowledge of behavioral health principles as they relate to developmental disabilities is highly desirable.
* Background in social work or related fields with relevant certifications or licenses preferred. Join us in making a meaningful difference! We are dedicated to empowering our team members through supportive leadership and opportunities for professional growth while delivering exceptional services that transform lives every day.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $125,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Employee discount
* Health insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Director of Operations and Accreditation
jobs.description:
Director of Operations and Accreditation


Position Summary: The Director of Operations \& Accreditation leads accreditation, data strategy, and operational functions for the LaPenta School of Business. This role partners with academic leadership to support continuous improvement, strategic growth, and compliance with AACSB standards


Duties and responsibilities:


* Manages the LaPenta School of Business AACSB accreditation and assessment processes
* Supports the continuous improvement processes of Iona University undergraduate and graduate
* business programs
* Collects, manages, and analyzes all data in the LSB’s faculty tracking system (Sedona)
* Collects, manages, and analyzes all assurance of learning data
* Collects, manages, and analyzes all intellectual contributions data
* Serves as ex officio on the LSB’s standing committees
* Assists faculty committee chairs in the management of their documents and processes
* Prepares and/or assists with the preparation of all statistical data as relates to program history growth and development. This includes special reports and presentations as needed by the Dean, Provost's Office, campus administrative offices, and external organizations such as AACSB International and US News \& World Report.
* Develops and coordinates all financial and budgetary matters as they relate to LSB programs
* Performs daily fiscal activities, maintenance and compliance of the University's internal accounting system and reporting requirements to the University’s financial offices
* Maintains inventory of all LSB office supplies, placing replenishment orders when needed
* Works with the Dean, Associate Dean, and chairs to support the development of new revenue generating programs
* Onboards all new LSB faculty and staff, in coordination with the Associate Dean of Faculty Affairs and Human Resources office
* Ensures all work related to the origination of payroll and payroll change forms for adjunct faculty and student assistants is completed
* Coordinates space in the LSB, including approving spaces in 25Live, and is the liaison with Facilities to address any deficiencies in the business building. Space coordination includes scheduling computer labs for classes.
* Serves as the liaison with the Information Technology office to address any challenges with technology in the LSB
* Performs other duties as assigned


Reports to: Dean, LaPenta School of Business


Education


Bachelor’s required; MBA or relevant master’s strongly preferred


Experience

* 5\+ years of progressively responsible experience in:
* Higher education administration
* Accreditation (AACSB strongly preferred)
* Data analytics / reporting
* Budget and operations management


Skills

* Strong analytical and data management skills
* Experience with accreditation systems and reporting tools
* Advanced Excel and/or data visualization tools
* Excellent project management and organizational skills
* Strong written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to collaborate with faculty and senior leadership

**Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.**

**About Iona/EEO Statement:**


Founded in 1940, Iona University is a private, Catholic, coeducational institution of learning in the tradition of the Edmund Rice Christian Brothers. Iona is a growing, highly accredited institution located just 20 miles north of Midtown Manhattan, with campuses in New Rochelle, Bronxville, and a new location in County Mayo, Ireland. With over 4,000 students, Iona offers small classes, engaged faculty, and a wide range of programs across the School of Arts \& Science, the LaPenta School of Business (AACSB accredited), the NewYork Presbyterian Iona School of Nursing \& Health Sciences, and the Hynes Institute for Entrepreneurship \& Innovation.


Recognized by The Princeton Review, The Wall Street Journal, Forbes, and U.S. News \& World Report, Iona is nationally ranked for academic quality, social mobility, and long term return on investment. The University’s continued growth and rising reputation reflect its commitment to academic excellence, innovation, service, and opportunity.


Iona University values and celebrates diversity and is committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive environment for all employees and applicants for employment. Iona is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered free from discrimination based on any legally protected characteristic under applicable federal, state, or local laws. Iona University encourages applications from qualified candidates of all backgrounds.

jobs.title: Program Director of Non-Profit Leadership and Community Partnerships
jobs.description:
* 496556
* Full\-Time
* Nonprofit Leadership Alliance
**Add to favorites** **View favorites****Department**: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance **Salary**: Commensurate with Experience/Education
**Description**:



Directs an ongoing university program to ensure that prescribed activities are carried out in accordance with its mission and/or specified objectives. Prepares short\- and long\-term goals and objectives.
  

  

1\. Plans, develops and administers methods, strategies and procedures for meeting program objectives; directs and oversees the daily activities of staff members.
  

2\. Prepares and monitors the annual budget and ensures that expenditures are within budget allocation.
  

3\. Directs and coordinates personally, or through subordinate personnel, activities concerned with carrying out the objectives of the program.
  

4\. Directs the preparation of and reviews reports and records of activities to ensure progress is being accomplished toward specified program objectives; modifies or changes methodology as required to redirect activities and attains objectives.
  

5\. Analyzes trends within program areas, maintaining expert awareness of program information and change needs.
  

6\. Formulates, implements, and maintains appropriate policies and procedures concerning program administration.
  

7\. Manages and administers grant for program, including meetings, communication coordination, scholarships and other operations.
  

8\. May prepare or participate in the preparation of proposals to obtain grants from state and federal agencies, and private foundation sponsors.
  

9\. Performs other job\-related duties as assigned.
  

  

**Additional Posting Information:**
  

The vision of the Graduate College of Social Work (GCSW) is shape the future of social work through innovative education, transformative research, and collaborative partnerships that advance workforce readiness, health, and community well\-being. Reporting to the Dean, the Director of Non\-Profit Leadership and Community Partnerships contributes to the vision by developing, implementing, and overseeing unique opportunities that enhance learning for students (undergraduate and graduate)at the University of Houston from any degree program who are interested in earning the Certified Non\-Profit Profession (CNP) credential through the Nonprofit Leadership Alliance (NLA) program . This is done in conjunction with faculty and staff from across the university as well as external partners throughout the greater Houston community and beyond. This position administers the Nonprofit Leadership Alliance program and represents the University locally, nationally, and globally to serve as a foundation for broader workforce development, experiential learning, employer engagement, and nonprofit innovation.  

  

Position Summary:
  

* Serves as a visionary and collaborative member of the Dean’s Executive Team committed to advancing the Strategic Priorities of the GCSW and goals of the NLA
* Hire and supervise the NLA Program Coordinator to ensure the quality execution of all day\-to\-day activities of the program
* Hire and manage a Graduate Research Assistant that will support the work and activities of the NLA Office
* Prepare, monitor, and manage annual program budget, including coordinating multiple funding streams and accounts
* Provide grant oversight and ensure any grant/funding requirements are met by the program
* Identify, cultivate, and solicit (through appropriate university channels) new donor partnerships
* Provide stewardship and collaborative engagement to previous and current donor partners
* Provide strategic leadership and direction for the NLA Advisory Board to advance program growth, community engagement, and workforce development.

  

Ensure the effective management, accuracy, and integrity of program records and documentation.  

Ensure the effective execution, administration, and oversight of program\-related contracts and agreements, including compliance with established terms and requirements.  

Develop, implement, and oversee policies and procedures that support program effectiveness, operational excellence, and sustained success.  

Cultivate and maintain strategic engagement with the NLA Leadership Circle, leveraging its advisory role to advance program priorities, strengthen community partnerships, and support sustained program growth.  

Lead the ongoing assessment of program performance and outcomes, ensuring alignment with University priorities and the national NLA affiliation agreement, and oversee the preparation of annual reports for University leadership, funders, and community stakeholders.  

Represent the NLA to internal and external constituents, cultivating strategic relationships and partnerships that advance program visibility, engagement, and impact.
  

* Other duties as assigned

  

Primary Responsibilities  

Program Leadership  

* Provide strategic leadership and oversight for all aspects of the Nonprofit Leadership Alliance (NLA) program.
* Develop and implement a strategic plan for program growth, student engagement, and community impact.
* Manage annual program goals, assessment, reporting, and continuous improvement activities.
* Ensure compliance with all Nonprofit Leadership Alliance program requirements and standards.

  

Student Success \& Workforce Development  

* Recruit, advise, mentor, and support students participating in the NLA.
* Develop leadership development programming that prepares students for careers in nonprofit management and community leadership.
* Expand workforce\-aligned learning opportunities, including internships, certifications, leadership academies, and professional development workshops.
* Coordinate career readiness programming including résumé reviews, interviewing, networking, and leadership skill development.
* Track student outcomes related to certification, employment, and leadership placement.

  

Community \& Employer Partners

jobs.title: Director of Advancement
jobs.description:
Naperville Community Television (NCTV17\) is seeking a relationship\-driven, entrepreneurial fundraising professional to serve as our next Director of Advancement.

This is an opportunity to shape a growing culture of philanthropy at one of the most trusted organizations in the community. As Naperville’s only nonprofit, community\-centered news source, NCTV17 provides free and accessible local journalism that keeps residents informed, connected, and engaged.

The Director of Advancement will help build the next chapter of NCTV17 by expanding philanthropic support, strengthening community partnerships, and helping ensure trusted local journalism remains free and accessible for future generations.

As the station approaches its 40th anniversary in 2027, NCTV17 enters a pivotal period of growth, visibility, and community engagement. The ideal candidate understands that local journalism is more than media — it is community infrastructure — and can communicate the impact of trusted local news in ways that inspire support.

**How you’ll make an impact**

Reporting directly to the Executive Director, the Director of Advancement is responsible for leading NCTV17’s fundraising efforts through major gifts, annual giving, grants, corporate partnerships, and special events in order to strengthen the organization’s long\-term financial sustainability.

This is a hands\-on role within a small nonprofit where success requires both strategic thinking and day\-to\-day execution. The successful candidate won’t inherit a large advancement department—they’ll help build one. Success will be measured not only by revenue growth, but by the strength of the donor relationships, fundraising systems, and culture of philanthropy they help create across the organization.

**Advancement Strategy \& Major Gifts**

* Partner with the Executive Director and Board to develop and execute a comprehensive fundraising strategy that grows philanthropic support and strengthens long\-term financial sustainability.
* Secure and steward annual leadership gifts of $1,000\+ through NCTV17’s *Director’s Circle* program, building a pipeline for future major gifts.

**Annual Giving \& Monthly Giving**

* Plan and execute an annual giving calendar focused on consistent donor communication and stewardship.
* Grow the *Channel Champions* monthly giving program to build sustainable revenue.
* Partner with Marketing to amplify NCTV17’s case for support in all communications.

**Grants \& Corporate Giving**

* Grow the grant portfolio through research, proposal development, submission, reporting, and tracking of grant requirements and deadlines.
* Cultivate and expand relationships with corporate partners, identifying opportunities for sponsorship, philanthropy, and long\-term community investment.

**Events \& Community Engagement**

* Partner with the program team to produce and scale NCTV17’s annual fundraiser, driving sponsorship growth, ticket sales, and donor engagement/support.
* Capitalize on NCTV17’s 40th anniversary in 2027 as a major opportunity for fundraising and community engagement.
* Represent NCTV17 within the community to deepen ties with donors, sponsors, and community leaders.

**Fundraising Operations**

* Supervise the Administrative Support Specialist in managing the Donor CRM using best practices for data entry, reporting, and standard operating procedures.
* Utilize fundraising analytics and performance metrics to assess effectiveness and inform strategy.

**The ideal candidate brings:**

* **Drive \& Accountability**: A self\-motivated, results\-oriented professional who is organized, detail\-oriented, and hard\-working.
* **Strategic Execution**: A creative collaborator comfortable balancing high\-level strategy with day\-to\-day execution.
* **Builder’s Mindset:** Motivated by the opportunity to build something meaningful, strengthen an advancement program, and help grow an organization making a measurable impact in the community.

**Qualifications**

* Education: Bachelor’s degree required.
* Experience: Minimum of 5\-7 years of nonprofit fundraising experience with demonstrated success cultivating, soliciting, and stewarding individual donors and growing philanthropic revenue.
* Fundraising Knowledge: Deep knowledge of industry best practices and a strategic ability to translate fundraising plans into measurable outcomes.
* Interpersonal Skills: Exceptional written, oral, and relationship\-building skills, with a demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with board members, staff, donors, sponsors, and diverse community leaders.
* Technical Skills: Experience working with donor CRM systems (DonorPerfect preferred), Google Workspace, and project management tools.
* Detail\-Oriented: Strong organizational and project management skills with exceptional attention to detail.
* Flexibility: Available to work occasional evenings and community events as needed.
* Location: Full\-time, in\-person position based in Naperville, Illinois with flexibility for some remote work.

**Apply**

* To apply, please send a resume and cover letter to: work@nctv17\.org. Please include “Director of Advancement” in the subject line.

Pay: From $75,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Position:** Executive Director

**Salary Range:** $110K \- $125K

**Location:** Alexandria, VA

**Apply at:** https://breastcancercollective.org/careers

**Lead the Next Chapter of The Breast Cancer Collective**

The Breast Cancer Collective is seeking an exceptional Executive Director to lead a respected and growing nonprofit organization dedicated to expanding access to breast cancer screening, diagnosis, education, and coordinated care.

Founded by breast surgeon, innovator, and entrepreneur **Dr. David Weintritt**, the Collective has spent more than a decade bringing together healthcare providers, nonprofit organizations, community leaders, philanthropic partners, and volunteers to remove barriers to care and improve outcomes for medically underserved communities.

Today, the organization stands at an exciting point in its evolution. Built on a decade of thoughtful leadership, trusted partnerships, and measurable community impact, the Breast Cancer Collective is well positioned for its next chapter of growth.

The Board of Directors is seeking an accomplished executive who has successfully built organizations, cultivated transformational partnerships, inspired philanthropic investment, and is energized by the opportunity to lead an organization where mission, innovation, and measurable impact come together.

This is an opportunity to build on a strong foundation while helping shape the future of breast health through collaboration, strategic leadership, and meaningful community partnerships.

**The Next Decade**

The Breast Cancer Collective has never measured success by the size of the organization, but by the lives it improves and the partnerships it builds.

Looking ahead, the Board envisions an organization that continues to expand its influence by deepening collaboration, accelerating innovation, and demonstrating measurable improvements in access to breast health services.

The next Executive Director will have the opportunity to help shape that future by:

* Expanding strategic partnerships with healthcare systems, physicians, employers, foundations, corporations, and community organizations.
* Strengthening philanthropic support through transformational donor relationships and innovative fundraising strategies.
* Leveraging data and measurable outcomes to demonstrate impact, inform decision\-making, and attract new investment.
* Serving as a trusted convener, bringing together organizations that can accomplish more collectively than independently.
* Positioning the Breast Cancer Collective as a recognized leader in improving equitable access to breast health services and reducing disparities in care.

The next chapter is not about becoming the largest organization. It is about becoming one of the most respected, collaborative, and impactful organizations advancing breast health.

**The Leadership Opportunity**

The Executive Director serves as the organization's chief executive and primary external leader. In partnership with the Board of Directors and Dr. Weintritt, the Executive Director will:

* Lead the organization's long\-term strategy and growth.
* Build enduring relationships with donors, healthcare leaders, community partners, foundations, and volunteers.
* Champion fundraising and long\-term financial sustainability.
* Serve as the organization's primary ambassador, cultivating partnerships that advance the mission and broaden the Collective's impact.

The role is intentionally designed to allow the Executive Director to focus on leadership, strategy, fundraising, and external relationships while partnering with an experienced Strategic Operations Lead responsible for operations, finance, technology, and organizational infrastructure.

**Strategic Priorities**

The Executive Director will lead implementation of the organization's strategic framework by advancing five priorities.

* **Expand Access:** Increase access to timely breast cancer screening, diagnosis, education, and coordinated care through innovative partnerships and community\-based initiatives.
* **Demonstrate Impact:** Use data, evaluation, and measurable outcomes to strengthen accountability, improve decision\-making, and clearly communicate organizational impact.
* **Build Sustainable Resources:** Expand philanthropic support through major gifts, grants, corporate partnerships, healthcare collaborations, and diversified fundraising strategies.
* **Strengthen the Collective:** Deepen relationships across healthcare systems, nonprofit organizations, volunteers, and community partners to build a stronger network of coordinated care.
* **Build Organizational Capacity:** Ensure the organization remains financially strong, operationally effective, and positioned for thoughtful, sustainable growth.

**The Ideal Candidate**

We are seeking a collaborative, entrepreneurial leader who enjoys building organizations through partnerships rather than hierarchy.

The successful candidate is equally comfortable meeting with corporate executives, major donors, foundation leaders, Board members, elected officials, volunteers, and community organizations. They earn trust quickly, communicate with credibility, and bring people together around a common purpose.

They are energized by building relationships, creating opportunities, and helping organizations reach their full potential. They are strategic without losing sight of execution, collaborative without avoiding difficult decisions, and confident enough to lead while remaining humble enough to listen.

While nonprofit leadership experience is valuable, we also welcome candidates from healthcare, philanthropy, academic medicine, higher education, or other mission\-driven organizations who have demonstrated exceptional leadership, fundraising success, and the ability to build lasting partnerships.

**Experience \& Qualifications**

The ideal candidate will bring:

* Significant executive leadership experience within a nonprofit organization, healthcare system, found

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR** 

* Reports to: Board of Directors
* Supervises: Operations Director

* Position Type: Full\-Time Position Summary

The Executive Director (ED) serves as the chief executive officer of Willow Rising, providing visionary, strategic, and financial leadership to advance the organization’s mission of supporting survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and related harm. In partnership with the Board of Directors, the ED ensures long\-term sustainability, strong governance, effective fundraising, and meaningful community partnerships. The ED provides executive oversight while delegating day\-to\-day operations and program management to the Operations Director. The Executive Director is not a primary direct\-service provider but maintains working knowledge of advocacy services and serves as an escalation point during organizational or community crises.

**Core Responsibilities** 

1\. Governance, Strategy \& Organizational Leadership (35%)

* Serve as chief executive and primary spokesperson for Willow Rising
* Partner with the Board of Directors on strategic planning and organizational direction
* Support effective board governance, development, and accountability
* Ensure mission alignment, ethical leadership, and organizational integrity
* Represent Willow Rising in high\-level community, systems, and coalition work
* Monitor organizational performance and adjust strategy as needed

2\. Financial Leadership \& Sustainability (30%)

* Provide fiduciary oversight of the organization’s financial health
* Review and approve all invoices and expenditures
* Lead annual budget development in partnership with the Operations Director and Board
* Oversee financial reporting, audits, and compliance
* Ensure strong internal controls and separation of duties
* Lead long\-term financial planning and sustainability efforts

3\. Fundraising, Development \& External Relations (25%)

* Lead organizational fundraising strategy
* Cultivate relationships with major donors, foundations, and funding partners
* Provide executive oversight of grant strategy and proposal development
* Serve as lead representative for fundraising events and donor engagement
* Strengthen community partnerships and visibility across the service area

4\. Executive Oversight \& Risk Management (10%)

* Supervise and support the Operations Director
* Provide executive oversight of compliance, contracts, and risk management
* Serve as escalation point for high\-risk or complex organizational matters

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in nonprofit management, public administration, human services, or related field (or equivalent experience)
* 5\+ years of nonprofit leadership experience
* Demonstrated success in fundraising and financial oversight
* Strong governance, communication, and leadership skills
* Commitment to survivor\-centered, trauma\-informed, and equitable practice

* Valid driver’s license and ability to travel within service area

**Preferred Qualifications** 

* Experience in domestic violence or sexual assault services
* Knowledge of Nebraska victim services systems
* Grant strategy and compliance experience
* Lived experience valued and welcomed
* Bilingual proficiency in English and Spanish

Benefits:

* Employee assistance program
* Paid time off

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Are you up for a challenge? Would you find yourself thriving by advocating for families whose homes and communities have been damaged or destroyed by disaster? This role is for someone who senses God's call to use fundraising as service and stewardship.**

**About FCDR**

Fuller Center Disaster ReBuilders (FCDR) is a Christian disaster rebuilding organization helping families recover after catastrophic loss through home rebuilding, volunteer mobilization, and long\-term community restoration. FCDR is entering a new season of growth as we strengthen fundraising capacity, expand donor relationships, and support future disaster recovery efforts and major initiatives such as the ROOTS Community Land Trust project in Western North Carolina.

**Position Summary**

We are seeking a mission\-driven Director of Development to lead fundraising strategy and donor engagement across major donors, churches, foundations, corporations, campaigns, recurring giving, and long\-term donor stewardship. This is a builder role. The Director of Development will collaborate closely with the Executive Director, board members, volunteers, and strategic partners to deepen relationships, strengthen fundraising systems, and build sustainable support for FCDR’s mission and future growth.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead and implement FCDR's development strategy and annual fundraising plan.
* Manage donor cultivation, solicitation, stewardship, and follow\-up.
* Build and manage major donor and prospect pipelines.
* Strengthening church, corporate, foundation, and community partnerships
* Support campaign fundraising efforts related to the ROOTS Community Land Trust initiative.
* Help implement and manage CRM, donor tracking, stewardship, and reporting systems.
* Coordinate donor communications, impact reports, and development materials.
* Partner with executive leadership and board members on fundraising strategy and donor engagement
* Support development dashboards, revenue forecasting, and board reporting.

**What Makes This Opportunity Unique**

FCDR combines a deeply meaningful mission, visible impact in disaster\-affected communities, strong volunteer engagement, authentic stories of transformation, and significant long\-term growth opportunities.The right candidate will help shape the next stage of FCDR’s development work while preserving the relational, mission\-centered culture that defines the organization. This is meaningful work connected to real families, real communities, and long\-term recovery after disaster. It requires resilience, adaptability, humility, initiative, and a willingness to carry responsibility during both demanding and rewarding seasons.

**The Kind of Person Who Will Thrive** 

The strongest candidates will be highly relational, self\-directed, organized, adaptable, resilient, emotionally mature, and motivated by mission and service. This role will resonate most deeply with someone who feels personally called by God toward mission\-driven service, relationship\-centered fundraising, and helping build long\-term impact for families recovering from disaster. The strongest fit will be someone energized by meaningful work, lasting impact, and the opportunity to help strengthen a mission that serves people in some of the hardest moments of their lives.

**Qualifications**

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience
* Minimum 5 years of experience in nonprofit fundraising, development, sales, partnership development, or related leadership roles
* Demonstrated relationship\-building and communication skills.
* Strong organizational and project management abilities
* Ability to manage multiple donor relationships and priorities simultaneously.
* Mature Christian faith and alignment with FCDR's Christian mission and values, including the ability to represent the organization's faith\-based mission with integrity, humility, and professionalism.

**Preferred**

* Experience in major gifts, donor stewardship, and capital campaign fundraising
* Experience with CRM systems and donor pipeline management
* Experience working with churches, faith\-based organizations, foundations, and community partners.
* Familiarity with disaster recovery, housing, community development, or volunteer\-driven nonprofit work
* Experience helping organizations strengthen fundraising systems or infrastructure.

**Location**

Position is remote. Residency in Atlantic or Gulf Coast state desirable.

**Reporting Relationship**

Responsible to: Executive Director

Pay: $75,000\.00 \- $85,000\.00 per year

Application Question(s):

* Do you currently live in an Atlantic or Gulf Coast state?
* This role requires mature Christian faith and the ability to represent FCDR’s Christian mission and values with integrity, humility, and professionalism. Are you comfortable with this requirement?
* Have you personally managed an assigned portfolio of major\-gift prospects and donors, with responsibility for cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship?
* Have you created and executed an annual fundraising plan spanning multiple revenue sources, such as major gifts, churches, foundations, corporations, or recurring giving?

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* personally soliciting and closing gifts from major donors: 5 years (Required)

Willingness to travel:

* 25% (Required)

Work Location: Remote

jobs.title: Program Director (Student Y)
jobs.description:
Learn more and apply at: https://www.kyymca.org/about/careers/

POSITION SUMMARY:

The Program Director (Student Y) is responsible for creating, coordinating, and implementing all aspects of the Student Y program. Student Y is a student\-led program for middle and high school students (grades 6–12\) interested in making an impact in local and global communities. Student Y provides schools and community organizations with opportunities to engage in meaningful service\-learning projects throughout the school year while developing compassionate, informed, and engaged community leaders.

This position is responsible for building and sustaining strong relationships with students, teachers, advisors, school administrators, alumni, community partners, and volunteers across the Commonwealth. The Program Director (Student Y) serves as a key representative of the organization within schools and communities by maintaining a regular presence in schools, directly supporting chapters, and collaborating with the Directors of Program Outreach to recruit and retain schools, advisors, students, and members for Student Y programs and initiatives.

The Program Director (Student Y) oversees the development and implementation of new classroom curriculum opportunities, service\-learning initiatives, leadership programming, and community engagement strategies that expand the reach of the Kentucky YMCA mission into schools and communities statewide. This includes working with partners to implement innovative resources and national programming initiatives through Student Y and other KY YMCA program areas where the organization is positioned to serve students both within Kentucky and beyond.

This role works closely with the Student Y Youth Council to plan and implement statewide service projects, voter registration and civic engagement efforts, youth workshops, and leadership opportunities throughout the year. The position serves as the lead for voter education, registration, and civic engagement initiatives associated with Student Y programming.

The Program Director (Student Y) is also responsible for assisting with the marketing and promotion of all aspects of the program, including creating new content when necessary and celebrating the impact of chapters, schools, and students through social media, storytelling, outreach campaigns, and press opportunities. In alignment with the organization’s strategic plan, this role contributes to planning and implementation efforts to increase revenue and participation for Student Y through programming growth, memberships, fundraising opportunities, merchandise strategies, and expanded school engagement.

This position oversees and supports the college\-level Student Y program, with a strong emphasis on relationship building and leadership development.

The Program Director (Student Y) collaborates closely with all program directors and departments across the organization to ensure alignment, consistency, and support across program areas. This position regularly serves as an on\-site director and implementer for Student Y and other KY YMCA programs, including but not limited to GFI, LTC, Y\-Corps, Youth in Government, Model United Nations, and national programs through YUSA. Responsibilities include leading and directing both staff and volunteer teams on\-site, overseeing implementation and logistics, managing the participant experience, and ensuring the successful execution of programs planned collaboratively with other directors and organizational leadership.

**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:**

1\. Develops, coordinates, implements, and evaluates all aspects of the Student Y program for middle and high school students across Kentucky.

2\. Builds and maintains strong relationships with students, teachers, advisors, school administrators, alumni, volunteers, and community partners to strengthen and expand Student Y participation statewide and nationally.

3\. Maintains a regular presence in schools and communities to support chapters, recruit new schools and participants, provide advisor support, and strengthen engagement with Student Y programming.

4\. Collaborates with the Directors of Program Outreach and other program staff to recruit, retain, and support Student Y members, schools, and advisors.

5\. Develops and implements new classroom and local community curriculum opportunities, leadership initiatives, service\-learning projects, and community\-based programming that furthers the mission and strategic goals of the Kentucky YMCA.

6\. Works with the Student Y Youth Council, student leaders, and college chapters to plan and implement service projects, civic engagement initiatives, voter registration efforts, leadership workshops, and statewide activities.

7\. Leads and supports voter education, voter registration, civic engagement, and advocacy initiatives connected to Student Y and related programming.

8\. Supports strategic planning efforts focused on increasing Student Y participation, program revenue, memberships, sponsorships, merchandise sales, and long\-term program sustainability.

9\. Identifies and implements strategies in collaboration with the Directors of Program Outreach to expand Student Y into new schools, communities, and regions throughout Kentucky and surrounding service areas.

10\. Assists with the creation, coordination, and execution of marketing and communications strategies for Student Y, including social media content, storytelling, press releases, promotional materials, and outreach campaigns.

11\. Collaborates with KY YMCA leadership, Director of Mission Advancement, and Program Directors to align Student Y efforts with broader organizational goals, statewide initiatives, and national YMCA programming priorities.

12\. Serves as an on\-site director and lead implementer for Student Y and other Kentucky YMCA programs and conferences, including but not limited to Youth in Government, Model United Nations, Go For It Conference (GFI), LTC, Y\-Co

jobs.title: Chief Program Officer
jobs.description:
**POSITION SUMMARY**

The Women’s and Children’s Alliance (WCA) is seeking a thoughtful, strategic, and mission\-driven leader to serve as its Chief Program Officer (CPO). This is a unique opportunity to lead and strengthen a comprehensive continuum of care for individuals impacted by domestic violence and sexual assault. At an important point in WCA’s growth, the CPO will guide the organization’s programmatic vision and ensure services across shelter, clinical care, case management, court advocacy, and childcare are fully integrated, trauma\-informed, and centered on a high\-quality client experience. This leader will play a key role in ensuring every individual served feels safe, respected, and supported, while advancing measurable outcomes that reflect meaningful impact.

As a member of the Executive Team, the CPO will partner closely with the CEO and other leaders to strengthen alignment across programs, operations, and community partnerships. This role offers the opportunity to build on a strong foundation—supporting and developing program leaders, enhancing service quality, and using data and insight to guide continuous improvement. The ideal candidate brings both strategic perspective and practical leadership, with a deep commitment to mission\-driven work and a collaborative approach to leading teams. This is an opportunity to make a lasting difference by helping shape how services are delivered and experienced—today and into the future.

**ESSENTIAL AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITY**

**Program Strategy \& Organizational Effectiveness \- 20%**

**Program \& Impact Oversight – 20%**

**Residential \& Community Program Leadership – 15%**

**People Leadership \& Culture – 15%**

**Compliance, Quality \& Risk Management – 10%**

**Financial \& Resource Stewardship – 10%**

**External Leadership \& Partnerships – 10%**

**CORE EXECUTIVE LEADER COMPETENCIES**

**Mission \& Values Leadership** – Champions and advances WCA’s mission, vision, and values by clearly articulating their meaning and ensuring they are reflected in strategy, decision\-making, and organizational culture. Models integrity and ethical leadership while holding self and others accountable to purpose\-driven outcomes

**Visionary \& Strategic Leadership** – Develops and advances forward\-looking strategies that align with organizational priorities and position WCA for long\-term success.

**Inclusive \& Collaborative Leadership** – Builds trust, fosters belonging, and engages diverse perspectives to drive shared decision\-making and strong team outcomes.

**Results Orientation \& Accountability** – Sets clear goals, drives execution, and holds self and others accountable for achieving measurable, mission\-driven results.

**Communication \& Influence** – Communicates with clarity and authenticity, inspiring others and effectively influencing internal and external stakeholders.

**Stakeholder \& Board Engagement** – Cultivates strong, productive relationships with the Board, donors, partners, and community stakeholders to advance organizational goals.

**Change Leadership** – Leads with adaptability and resilience, guiding individuals and teams through change with clarity, empathy, and focus.

**JOB\-SPECIFIC COMPETENCIES – CHIEF PROGRAM OFFICER**

**Program Strategy \& Impact Leadership** – Designs and executes program strategies that drive measurable, mission\-aligned outcomes

**Organizational Effectiveness** – Strengthens systems, structures, and cross\-functional alignment to improve performance and impact

**Trauma\-Informed Leadership** – Applies deep understanding of domestic violence and sexual assault services to guide programs and teams

**Client Experience \& Outcomes Focus** – Ensures services are designed and delivered to maximize client safety, satisfaction, accessibility, and long\-term outcomes

**Service Integration \& Quality** – Ensures seamless, high\-quality coordination across all service areas

**Data \& Evaluation Leadership** – Uses data, metrics, and evaluation frameworks to inform decisions and demonstrate impact

**People \& Culture Leadership** – Builds strong, accountable teams and fosters a collaborative, mission\-driven culture

**External Advocacy \& Partnerships** – Represents WCA effectively and builds relationships that enhance impact and resources

**Compliance \& Risk Management** – Ensures adherence to regulatory, licensing, and funding requirements

**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS**

**Education**

* Master’s degree in Social Work, Counseling, Psychology, Public Administration, or related field required
* LCSW or LCPC licensure in Idaho (or ability to obtain) strongly preferred

**Experience**

* 7\+ years of progressive leadership experience in human services, behavioral health, or nonprofit programs with at least 3 years in senior leadership roles with oversight of multiple programs or service lines; preferably in a nonprofit or mission\-driven organization
* Experience leading or supporting 24/7 residential or crisis\-based services strongly preferred

**Program \& Operational Leadership Experience**

* Demonstrated experience overseeing direct service programs and multidisciplinary teams
* Experience managing programs within regulated, licensed, or grant\-funded environments
* Experience with program performance monitoring, reporting, or outcome tracking
* Experience collaborating cross\-functionally with finance, development, or operations functions

**Additional Requirements**

* Must possess and maintain a valid driver’s license and meet State of Idaho requirements
* Successful completion of background check
* Ability to travel locally for meetings and events

**Salary Range** $83,700 \- $93,700

*The Women's \& Children's Alliance (WCA) one of the most well regarded non\-profits organizations in the valley is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in its hiring practices or any other employment decisions on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, age, sex,

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer
jobs.description:
**Position Title:** Chief Executive Officer

**Organization:** Advancing Connecticut Together

**Location:** Hartford, Connecticut (Hybrid)

**Reports to:** Board of Directors

**Status:** Full Time, Exempt

**Mission:** Advancing Connecticut Together (ACT), with its partners, addresses the root causes of poverty, addiction, and health inequities through strength\-based services and advocacy to ensure all people in Connecticut have equitable resources necessary to achieve multi\-generational health, wealth, and happiness.

**About Us:** With a statewide footprint, ACT provides case management, emergency financial assistance, HIV services, housing support, financial empowerment, tax preparation, advocacy, and serves as a trusted fiscal and administrative partner across Connecticut’s human services ecosystem.

After twenty\-eight years of stable leadership, ACT seeks a new Chief Executive Officer to guide the organization through its next chapter of sustainability, growth, and impact.

**Position Summary:** The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) is the chief executive and organizational leader responsible for advancing ACT’s mission, strengthening financial sustainability, stewarding relationships, supporting staff and culture, and partnering with the Board on strategy and governance.

The CEO will inherit a strong, mission\-driven $12\.9M organization with complex funding, deep public\-sector relationships, and a highly experienced leadership team. This role calls for a people\-centered, financially savvy, and relational leader who can lead its 40\-member staff through transition while honoring ACT’s culture and history.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Organizational Leadership \& Culture**

* Lead ACT with integrity, transparency, and a commitment to racial and health equity
* Foster a supportive, inclusive, and high\-performing workplace culture
* Develop and retain a strong senior leadership team
* Ensure clear communication, accountability, and staff engagement

**Financial Management, Public Funding \& Grants Management Expertise**

* Steward multi\-program budgets and public contracts
* Understand compliance\-heavy funding environments
* Partner effectively with finance leadership and Board
* Use financial data for strategic decision\-making

**External Relations \& Advocacy**

* Communicate ACT’s intersections of health, housing, poverty, and justice in ways that inform, engage, and influence
* Foster relationships with state agencies, funders, and partners
* Serve as a key ambassador to policymakers, funders, and community leaders
* Represent ACT in advocacy efforts aligned with mission and values
* Ensure organizational readiness and steady leadership amid evolving federal and state funding landscapes

**Strategy, Systems \& Program Oversight**

* Lead the organization through executive leadership transition following long\-tenured leadership, while providing clarity, continuity, and readiness for future strategic planning.
* Assess program alignment, scale, and sustainability
* Strengthen internal systems, documentation, and cross\-program coordination
* Monitor risks and ensure effective operational performance

**Fundraising \& Revenue Diversification**

* Align fundraising strategy with mission, impact, and data
* Lead a diversified revenue strategy that strengthens unrestricted funding beyond public sources, while avoiding competition for private dollars relied upon by partner organizations

**Board Partnership \& Governance**

* Work collaboratively with the Board of Directors
* Support the Board in governance, strategic thinking, and leadership development
* Provide clear reporting on finances, programs, and organizational health

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Demonstrates a sustained commitment to racial and health equity through inclusive leadership, equitable decision\-making, culturally responsive services, and advocacy to address structural barriers affecting the communities ACT serves.
* Brings senior leadership experience in direct service or human services nonprofits, with a track record of guiding mission\-driven teams and overseeing client\-centered operations of comparable scale and complexity.
* Offers deep expertise in managing government contracts and public funding streams, including compliance, reporting, and long\-term relationship stewardship with federal, state, and local funders.
* Provides experience in financial stewardship across complex, multi\-program budgets, balancing fiscal sustainability with programmatic impact and mission priorities.
* Has a proven record of fundraising and revenue diversification, strengthening a mix of public, private, institutional, and unrestricted funding sources.
* Leads organizations effectively through periods of transition or growth, offering stability, clarity, and thoughtful change management during times of evolution.
* Cultivates strong, trust\-based relationships within public\-sector and nonprofit ecosystems, advancing collaboration, systems alignment, and shared community impact.
* Brings familiarity with ACT’s intersecting issue areas—including health equity, HIV, housing, poverty, and addiction—and the ability to connect programs, policy, and advocacy to meaningful outcomes.

**Compensation and Benefits:**

**Salary Range:** $150,000 \- $180,000 depending on experience and qualifications.

* Comprehensive health and dental insurance
* Paid short\-term and long\-term disability insurance, 1x salary term life insurance
* 3% employer retirement match
* Paid Time Off: 30 days plus 9 Federal holidays and 3 floating holidays
* Hybrid, flexible and collaborative workplace (currently 2 days in\-office minimum)

**To apply:**

ACT is partnering with Fio Partners to support and manage the Chief Executive Officer search process. To ensure a confidential and structured process, all candidates should apply through our applicant system here:

https://fiopartners.zohorecruit.com/jobs/Careers/812254000001137002/Advancing\-CT\-Tog
